title: RHCSA 10 Mock Exam G
description: Recovery + server administration focus: root password recovery, server-side login policy, process management, file search, systemd timers, swap, and LVM storage.

tasks: (client) The root password has been lost. Boot into emergency mode and reset the root password to cinder9.
(client) Set the hostname to clientg.exam10.lab. Add an entry to /etc/hosts mapping 192.168.122.3 to serverg.exam10.lab.
(client) Configure the connection "System eth1" with static IPv4: address 192.168.122.66/24, gateway 192.168.122.1, DNS 192.168.122.3. The connection must start automatically.
(client) Add the kernel boot argument audit_backlog_limit=8192 to the default GRUB entry so it persists across reboots.
On client and server, create enabled BaseOS and AppStream repository definitions with BaseOS at http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and AppStream at http://server/repo/AppStream/; disable GPG checks.

On client, add a system-level Flatpak remote named examgflatpak pointing to file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo with GPG verification disabled. Install org.rhcsa.Tools from that remote and leave it installed.
(server) Set the server login message in /etc/motd to Authorized exam-g server.
(client) Create group devg10. Create users grant10 and hazel10 with devg10 as a supplementary group and passwords set to cinder9.
(client) Create directory /srv/devg10 owned by root:devg10 with permissions 1770 (setgid and sticky bit, no world access).
(client) Create user noaccess70 with no home directory and login shell /sbin/nologin.
(client) Set password aging for grant10: maximum 35 days, minimum 5 days, warning 7 days. Add umask 0077 to /home/grant10/.bashrc.
(client) Create user copy10 with UID 5010 and password cinder9 on the client. Also create user copy10 with the same UID 5010 and password cinder9 on the server.
(client) As copy10, generate an SSH key pair (no passphrase) and distribute the public key to copy10@server. Then copy /etc/hostname from the server to /home/copy10/server-hostname on the client.
(client) Schedule an at job for user hazel10 that runs: echo "exam-g task" >> /home/hazel10/at-result.txt
(server) Configure persistent systemd journal storage on the server.
On server, route local5 log messages to /var/log/server-g-local5.log and write a test message.
On server, create /srv/serverg10 owned by root:serverg10 with mode 2770.
On server, create group serverg10 and user srvg10 with password cinder9, then add the user to serverg10.
On server, publish /var/www/html/server-g.html containing RHCSA10-G and serve httpd on TCP port 8206.
On server, create and enable servergtimer.timer so it appends SERVER-G to /var/log/servergtimer.log every 12 minutes.
(client) Create a 500 MiB swap partition on /dev/sdb, format it as swap, and enable it persistently via /etc/fstab.
(client) Create physical volume on /dev/sdc, volume group vgg10, logical volume datag of 300 MiB, format as XFS, and mount persistently at /mnt/datag10.
On server, export /exports/exam-g to the 192.168.122.0/24 network. On client, mount server:/exports/exam-g persistently at /mnt/gprojects.
hints: Make changes persistent unless a question says otherwise.
Use local repositories and local documentation; do not depend on internet access.
Flatpak and systemd timer tasks belong to the RHCSA 10 track.
Task 1 requires rebooting into a rescue target to reset the root password.
Tasks marked (server) must be performed on the server VM.
checks: echo cinder9 | su - root -c 'whoami' 2>/dev/null | grep -qx root
hostnamectl --static | grep -qx clientg.exam10.lab && grep -Eq '^192\.168\.122\.3[[:space:]]+serverg\.exam10\.lab$' /etc/hosts

connection_name="System eth1"; nmcli -g NAME connection show "$connection_name" >/dev/null 2>&1 || connection_name="$(private_dev="$(ip -o -4 addr show | awk '$4 ~ /^192\.168\.122\./ {print $2; exit}')"; nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: -v private_dev="$private_dev" '$2 == private_dev {print $1; exit}')"; test -n "$connection_name" && nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.66/24' && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.1' && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx '192.168.122.3' && nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "$connection_name" | grep -qx manual
grep -q 'audit_backlog_limit=8192' /proc/cmdline || grubby --info=DEFAULT | grep -q 'audit_backlog_limit=8192'

awk 'function flush(){if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/BaseOS\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") base=1;if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/AppStream\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") app=1}function value(){sub(/^[^=]*=/, ""); gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, ""); return $0}/^[[:space:]]*\[/ {flush(); baseurl=enabled=gpgcheck=""; next}/^[[:space:]]*baseurl[[:space:]]*=/ {baseurl=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*enabled[[:space:]]*=/ {enabled=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*gpgcheck[[:space:]]*=/ {gpgcheck=value(); next}END{flush(); exit !(base && app)}' /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; awk '"'"'function flush(){if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/BaseOS\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") base=1;if(baseurl ~ /^http:\/\/server\/repo\/AppStream\/?$/ && enabled == "1" && gpgcheck == "0") app=1}function value(){sub(/^[^=]*=/, ""); gsub(/^[ \t]+|[ \t]+$/, ""); return $0}/^[[:space:]]*\[/ {flush(); baseurl=enabled=gpgcheck=""; next}/^[[:space:]]*baseurl[[:space:]]*=/ {baseurl=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*enabled[[:space:]]*=/ {enabled=value(); next}/^[[:space:]]*gpgcheck[[:space:]]*=/ {gpgcheck=value(); next}END{flush(); exit !(base && app)}'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/*.repo'

flatpak remotes --system --columns=name,url 2>/dev/null | awk '$1 == "examgflatpak" && $2 == "file:///opt/rhcsa/flatpak/repo" {found=1} END {exit !found}' && flatpak list --system --app --columns=application 2>/dev/null | grep -qx org.rhcsa.Tools
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -qx '"'"'Authorized exam-g server'"'"' /etc/motd'
getent group devg10 >/dev/null && id -nG grant10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx devg10 && id -nG hazel10 | tr ' ' '\n' | grep -qx devg10
test -d /srv/devg10 && stat -c '%a' /srv/devg10 | grep -qx 1770 && stat -c '%G' /srv/devg10 | grep -qx devg10 && stat -c '%U' /srv/devg10 | grep -qx root
id noaccess70 >/dev/null 2>&1 && getent passwd noaccess70 | grep -q '/sbin/nologin' && ! test -d /home/noaccess70
chage -l grant10 | grep -Eq 'Maximum.*35' && chage -l grant10 | grep -Eq 'Minimum.*5' && chage -l grant10 | grep -Eq 'warning.*7' && grep -q 'umask 0077' /home/grant10/.bashrc
id -u copy10 | grep -qx 5010
test -f /home/copy10/server-hostname && test -s /home/copy10/server-hostname
test -f /home/hazel10/at-result.txt || atq 2>/dev/null | grep -q .

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; files=""; test -d /var/log/journal && systemctl is-active systemd-journald | grep -qx active && files=$(find /etc/systemd -maxdepth 2 \( -path /etc/systemd/journald.conf -o -path '"'"'/etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*.conf'"'"' \) -type f 2>/dev/null); test -n "$files" && awk '"'"'FNR == 1 {in_journal=0} /^[[:space:]]*\[Journal\][[:space:]]*($|#)/ {in_journal=1; next} /^[[:space:]]*\[/ {in_journal=0} in_journal && /^[[:space:]]*Storage[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*persistent[[:space:]]*($|#)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' $files'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Eq '"'"'^local5\.\*[[:space:]]+/var/log/server-g-local5\.log$'"'"' /etc/rsyslog.d/server-g-local5.conf && systemctl is-active rsyslog | grep -qx active && grep -Fq '"'"'server-g-local5'"'"' /var/log/server-g-local5.log'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getent group serverg10 >/dev/null && stat -c '"'"'%U:%G:%a'"'"' /srv/serverg10 | grep -qx root:serverg10:2770'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getent group serverg10 >/dev/null && id -nG srvg10 | tr '"'"' '"'"' '"'"'\n'"'"' | grep -qx serverg10'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq RHCSA10-G /var/www/html/server-g.html && grep -Eq '"'"'^Listen[[:space:]]+8206$'"'"' /etc/httpd/conf.d/exam-g-port.conf && semanage port -l | awk '"'"'$1 == "http_port_t" && $2 == "tcp" && $0 ~ /(^|[ ,])8206([, ]|$)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' && firewall-cmd --permanent --query-port=8206/tcp && firewall-cmd --query-port=8206/tcp && systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl is-enabled servergtimer.timer | grep -qx enabled && grep -Eq '"'"'^OnCalendar=\*:0/12$'"'"' /etc/systemd/system/servergtimer.timer && grep -Fxq '"'"'echo SERVER-G >> /var/log/servergtimer.log'"'"' /usr/local/sbin/servergtimer.sh'

swap_uuid="$(blkid -s UUID -o value /dev/sdb1)"; test -n "$swap_uuid" && swapon --show=NAME --noheadings | grep -qx /dev/sdb1 && awk -v dev="/dev/sdb1" -v uuid="$swap_uuid" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == "swap" && $3 == "swap" && ($1 == dev || $1 == "UUID=" uuid || $1 == "/dev/disk/by-uuid/" uuid) {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab

lv_path=/dev/vgg10/datag; mapper_path=/dev/mapper/vgg10-datag; mountpoint=/mnt/datag10; lv_uuid="$(blkid -s UUID -o value "$lv_path")"; lvs "$lv_path" >/dev/null 2>&1 && findmnt -no TARGET "$mountpoint" | grep -qx "$mountpoint" && awk -v dev="$lv_path" -v mapper="$mapper_path" -v uuid="$lv_uuid" -v target="$mountpoint" '$1 !~ /^#/ && $2 == target && $3 == "xfs" && ($1 == dev || $1 == mapper || $1 == "UUID=" uuid || $1 == "/dev/disk/by-uuid/" uuid) {found=1} END {exit !found}' /etc/fstab

findmnt -no SOURCE,TARGET /mnt/gprojects | grep -qx 'server:/exports/exam-g /mnt/gprojects' && grep -Eq '^server:/exports/exam-g[[:space:]]+/mnt/gprojects[[:space:]]+nfs([[:space:]]|$)' /etc/fstab && ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Eq "^/exports/exam-g[[:space:]]+192\\.168\\.122\\.0/24" /etc/exports.d/exam-g-integrated.exports && systemctl is-active nfs-server | grep -qx active'
solution_outline: Work through each question independently and verify before moving on.
Complete the root password recovery first since it requires a reboot.
Set up networking and repos early so server-side and package tasks can succeed.
Create users on both client and server before configuring SSH keys.

solution_commands: # Reboot, interrupt GRUB, append rd.break to kernel line
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.66/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa10-exam.repo <<'EOF'
[rhcsa10-exam-baseos]
name=RHCSA10 Exam BaseOS
baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[rhcsa10-exam-appstream]
name=RHCSA10 Exam AppStream
baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
EOF

cat > /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa10-exam.repo <<'EOF'
[rhcsa10-exam-baseos]
name=RHCSA10 Exam BaseOS
baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0

[rhcsa10-exam-appstream]
name=RHCSA10 Exam AppStream
baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/
enabled=1
gpgcheck=0
EOF
su - hazel10 -c 'echo "echo \"exam-g task\" >> /home/hazel10/at-result.txt" | at now + 1 minute'
semanage port -a -t http_port_t -p tcp 8206 2>/dev/null || semanage port -m -t http_port_t -p tcp 8206

cat > /etc/systemd/system/servergtimer.service <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Server G timer job

[Service]
Type=oneshot
ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/servergtimer.sh
EOF

cat > /etc/systemd/system/servergtimer.timer <<'EOF'
[Unit]
Description=Run server G timer job

[Timer]
OnCalendar=*:0/12
Persistent=true

[Install]
WantedBy=timers.target
EOF
parted -s /dev/sdb -- mklabel gpt mkpart primary linux-swap 1MiB 501MiB
